rulings.Case Title: GOOEY "GEORGE" OCK ENG VS. ALICE EUBANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Alice Eubank'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 26, 2014, line 1. Defendant Alice Eubank'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication DENIED. Defendant has not demonstrated that there are no triable issues of fact. First, for each cause of action, Ms. Eubanks has not demonstrated that the statute of limitations bars each cause of action because she fails to address her alleged acts of conversion and fraud in May and June 2011. (Complaint, 18, 22 - 24.) Plaintiff Gooey "George" Ock Eng filed this action on May 17, 2013, which is within two years of these alleged acts. Second, for first cause of action, moving party has not proffered material facts in support of all of the grounds stated in the Notice of Motion. (Notice of Motion, p. 2:12-14, See UMF nos. 1 - 20.) Third, for the second cause of action, Defendant fails to address all allegations of fraud and concealment. (Complaint, 39(e).)= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GOOEY "GEORGE" OCK ENG VS. ALICE EUBANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Jimmy Eng And Judy Jong'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 26, 2014, line 1. Cross Defendants Jimmy Eng And Judy Jong'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to George Eng only as to 14th cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Alice Eubank Deposition p. 216, ln. 22-25. Motion is DENIED as to Issue #1 (causes of action 2, 3, 8, 9, and 10) - Issues raised by a summary judgment motion are framed by the pleadings. Moving party failed to meet its initial burden and to completely dispose of a cause of action. CCP 437c(f)(1). These causes of action are not based solely on alleged contract to convey ownership. See 31, 35, 66, 22, etc. Motion is DENIED as to Issue #2 (causes of action 2, 8, and 1)- triable issue of fact as to breach of 2005 contract. Alice Eubank Deposition p. 78, ln. 8-21. Motion is DENIED as to Issue # 3 (causes of action 6, 8, 9, 10, 14, and 15) - triable issue of fact as to the date of statute of limitations accrual. 2010 when Alice Eubank was allegedly forced to stop performing her duties under 2005 Agreement or 2013 when property was conveyed to Jimmy Eng. Motion is DENIED as to issue #5 (causes of action 14 and 15), except as indicated above - triable issue of fact as to extreme emotional distress. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLIE BARRETT et al VS. JEANMAIRE HRYSHKO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 26, 2014, line 2. rulings.Case Title: PAUL BOSCHETTI VS. DANIEL O'BLENIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Court'S Oct-182013 Judgment In Favor Of Pltf And Order Directing Clerk To Pay Defts Filing Fee
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 26, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT DANIEL O'BLENIS' Motion To Set Aside Court'S Oct-182013 Judgment In Favor Of Plaintiff And Order Direcgins Clerk To Pay Defts Filing Fee The Court does not find that October 18, 2013 judgment was void for lack of jurisdiction. Judge Wilken's August 14, 2013 pre-filing order applied to bankruptcy court filings as explained in judge Wilken's 2/12/14 order. Therefore, Andrisani v. Saugus Colony Limited (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 517 is not applicable. This Court does not have jurisdiction to rule on Defendant's motion due to pending appeal. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VALERIE LEE VS. MICHAEL W. SPENCER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Ntc Of Fmtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Discovery And Req. For Monetary Sanctions

rulings.Case Title: SYLVESTER SUN VS. SHAW XIANGFA ZHU
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 26, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT SHAW ZHU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR, case settled. Moving party's counsel is warned for the last time that persistent failure to comply with SFLR courtesy copies requirements will result in motions being taken off calendar. See Minute orders in cases 643869, 643780, 646185. Counsel again failed to provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint as required by SFLR 2.6C. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DIAMOND HEIGHTS SHOPPING CENTER, INC A DELAWARE VS. SAI CHEUNG CHU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 26, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANTS SAI CHU and LI LIANG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS DENIED. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer conferring jurisdiction on 5-day summons. Moving party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/REQ)
